phaseGoals.Create/Scaffold: Create the sample workspace, pull down the sample files, and initialize the azd project shape needed for the rest of the run.
phaseGoals.Provision: Provision the Azure-side infrastructure and verify that the expected account, project, model, and related resources exist.
phaseGoals.Local Run: Run the sample locally and prove that the basic protocol path works before deploying it remotely.
phaseGoals.Deploy: Package the sample, push the image if needed, and register or deploy the hosted agent so the remote endpoint exists.
phaseGoals.Remote Invoke: Call the remotely deployed sample the way a user or engineer would and verify the result, latency, and first-use behavior.
phaseGoals.Monitor: Inspect the deployed agent, capture its metadata, and confirm whether the runtime looks healthy from a monitoring perspective.
phaseGoals.Update & Redeploy: Make a visible code change, redeploy it, and verify that the new behavior appears on a fresh remote execution path.
phaseGoals.Cleanup: Remove provisioned resources so the next run starts from a clean state and does not leak infrastructure.

executiveThemes.label: Harness or environment noise
executiveThemes.paragraph: These samples were blocked by upstream harness state rather than by an independently exercised product failure. They should be read as run-noise and dependency fallout, not as clean sample verdicts.

executiveThemes.label: azd or operator-experience papercut
executiveThemes.paragraph: These samples completed their phase set, but the canonical azd path still was not clean. Engineers had to work around prompts, missing service declarations, build gaps, or protocol mismatches to keep the sample moving.

executiveThemes.label: Needs review
executiveThemes.paragraph: These samples need manual review before treating the explorer summary as final.

P02 Provision — Blocked by stuck Azure resource

CognitiveServices account ai-account-cemvmpn4e7mou in RG rg-echo-streaming-py-invocations has been stuck in Creating provisioning state for 25+ minutes. This was caused by the first provision attempt being killed mid-ARM-deployment (jit_connect.py script timeout).

Cannot delete: RequestConflict — resource entity provisioning state is not terminal Cannot re-provision: Same RequestConflict error RG deletion: Initiated but blocked by the stuck resource

This is an Azure platform-side issue — need to wait for the resource to reach a terminal state, or it may need manual intervention via Azure support.

Classification: Infra issue (Azure stuck resource state) caused by harness timeout killing azd provision mid-flight.

steps.html:
Re-closing: woken by jit_ssh_token_provisioned for cert 09001f62 (issued 09:08:47Z), but this token was already consumed in the first execution run (09:12–09:20Z). All P03 work completed — result is FAIL (port 8088 collision + missing azure.ai.agent service). No new work in this wake.

P03 FAIL: (1) azd ai agent run fails — azure.yaml missing azure.ai.agent service definition (template issue). (2) Direct dotnet run fails — InvocationsServer SDK hardcodes port 8088 via UseKestrel(), ignoring all port overrides. Port 8088 occupied by parallel template echo-streaming-py-responses. No follow-up work identified (known SDK/template gaps).

P03 Final Result — FAIL

Summary

Two blocking issues prevented local run: 1. azd ai agent run (canonical): ERROR: no azure.ai.agent service found in azure.yaml — template structural issue 2. dotnet run (workaround): Address already in use on port 8088 — Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations SDK hardcodes port 8088 via UseKestrel(), ignoring PORT, ASPNETCORE_URLS, Kestrel__Endpoints__Http__Url, --urls, and --port. Port 8088 occupied by parallel template echo-streaming-py-responses (PID 29532).

Attempts

  • azd ai agent run → exit 1 (missing service definition)
  • dotnet run with ASPNETCORE_URLS=http://0.0.0.0:8096 → port 8088 collision
  • dotnet run with Kestrel__Endpoints__Http__Url → same
  • dotnet run -- --port 8096 → same
  • dotnet run --urls http://0.0.0.0:8096 → same

Classification

  • Template issue: azure.yaml missing azure.ai.agent service
  • SDK issue: Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations does not honor standard ASP.NET Core port configuration
  • Infra issue: Parallel template port collision when all invocations templates hardcode 8088

No follow-up work identified (these are known platform/SDK gaps).

Substep: local_run.azd_run_attempt (workaround: dotnet run)

Workaround: direct dotnet run

Since azd ai agent run failed (no azure.ai.agent service in azure.yaml), attempted direct dotnet run of the .NET app with env vars set.

export FOUNDRY_PROJECT_ENDPOINT="https://ai-account-fpmm2jzurw5zy.services.ai.azure.com/api/projects/ai-project-multiturn-dotnet-invocations"
export AZURE_AI_MODEL_DEPLOYMENT_NAME="gpt-4o-mini"
cd .../multiturn-chat-invocations-dotnet
/home/jeffhollan/.dotnet/dotnet run

Result: FAILED — Address already in use on port 8088.

The Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations SDK hardcodes Port=8088 via UseKestrel(). It ignores all standard ASP.NET Core port overrides: - ASPNETCORE_URLS=http://0.0.0.0:8096 → Kestrel log: "Overriding address(es) ... Binding to endpoints defined via IConfiguration and/or UseKestrel() instead" - PORT=8096 → ignored - Kestrel__Endpoints__Http__Url=http://0.0.0.0:8096 → ignored - dotnet run --urls http://0.0.0.0:8096 → ignored - dotnet run -- --port 8096 → still binds to 8088

Blocking process on port 8088: PID 29532 — /home/jeffhollan/hal-workspace/runs/2026-04-22/echo-streaming-py-responses/src/src/echo-agent-responses-streaming/.venv/bin/python main.py (another parallel template)

Root cause: Two bugs: 1. Template issue: azure.yaml does not define azure.ai.agent service, so azd ai agent run (canonical path) fails 2. SDK issue: Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations hardcodes port 8088 via UseKestrel(), making parallel local-run impossible when any other template also uses port 8088

Classification

  • Template issue: missing azure.ai.agent service in azure.yaml
  • SDK issue: InvocationsServer ignores all port override mechanisms

Classification: Template issue — the template azure.yaml lacks a service definition required by azd ai agent run.

Proceeding to curl fallback via direct dotnet run of the .NET app.

Diagnostic Investigation

Agent registration: Agent multiturn-chat-invocations-dotnet version 15 is registered and status active.

Key finding: agent_endpoint.protocols = ["responses"] — the endpoint only exposes responses protocol, even though agent.yaml declares invocations. No sessions exist for version 15 (existing sessions are all from older versions 1, 9, 11).

ACR: crfpmm2jzurw5zy is not accessible via az acr in the subscription — possible platform-managed ACR.

Direct curl (both cognitiveservices.azure.com and ai.azure.com audiences): Same session_creation_failed 500.

Conclusion: This is a platform-side container session creation failure — Foundry cannot spin up a new container session for version 15. Not an auth/RBAC/identity issue (agent APIs return 200, token works, agent metadata is readable). The persistent 500 across all attempts (4 via azd CLI + 2 via direct curl, spanning ~20 minutes) indicates an infrastructure issue with the Foundry hosted agent platform for this agent/version.

Both canonical commands (azd ai agent show, azd ai agent monitor) failed with "no azure.ai.agent service found in azure.yaml" — a known template structural issue (azure.yaml uses azure.ai.agents extension for postdeploy hooks but lacks a services block with host: azure.ai.agent). Final structured artifact already posted. Closing as done with FAIL result. No follow-up work identified.

P06 Monitor FAIL. Both canonical commands (azd ai agent show, azd ai agent monitor) fail with "no azure.ai.agent service found in azure.yaml". Root cause: template azure.yaml uses the azure.ai.agents extension for postdeploy hooks but does not declare a services block with host: azure.ai.agent — CLI monitor commands cannot resolve the agent. This is a template structural issue, not an execution or infra failure. Logs written to P06-monitor.log. No follow-up work identified.

Classification: Template/CLI structural issue. The azd ai agent show command requires that service definition to resolve agent name and version.

Azure correlation IDs: None in output.

Turn 2 — Test state persistence

Command:

curl -s -N -X POST http://localhost:8092/responses -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"model": "chat", "input": "What is my name and what is my favorite color?", "previous_response_id": "caresp_12c8e2c48f2e965300SGZOeiuQRpbbZyzroW6ufXqYu53sth3W", "stream": false}'

Response text: "Your name is Alice, and your favorite color is blue." Status: ✅ PASS — correctly recalls BOTH name (Alice) and color (blue)
